  • Hide All Code

ERT Pilot — Exploratory Results

Guido Maria Caruso

2026-03-31

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(car)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

Overview

Pilot data from the ERT task (false cardiac feedback paradigm).

Key design choices:

  • Dependent variable: VAS intensity rating (−100 to +100)
  • Valence: positive (happiness) vs negative (anger + fear)
  • Within-subject factor: condition (synchronous / faster / slower)
  • Temporal covariate: trial position in block (1–6, mean-centred) to capture the exposure effect
  • Covariate: stimulus intensity
df <- list.files(path = ".", pattern = "FULL\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE) |>
  map_dfr(read_csv, show_col_types = FALSE,
          col_types = cols(participant_id = col_character())) |>
  filter(segment_type == "main") |>
  mutate(
    participant_id  = as.factor(participant_id),
    condition       = factor(condition,
                             levels = c("synchronous", "faster", "slower")),
    emotion         = factor(emotion,
                             levels = c("happiness", "anger", "fear")),
    valence         = factor(ifelse(emotion == "happiness",
                                   "positive", "negative"),
                             levels = c("positive", "negative")),
    trial_in_mini   = as.numeric(trial_in_mini),
    trial_in_mini_c = trial_in_mini - mean(trial_in_mini, na.rm = TRUE),
    intensity       = as.numeric(intensity),
    vas_value       = as.numeric(vas_value),
    rt              = as.numeric(rt)
  )

N participants: 7

1. Trial balance

table(df$condition, df$valence) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rename(Condition = Var1, Valence = Var2, N = Freq) |>
  kable(caption = "Trial count per condition × valence") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Trial count per condition × valence
Condition Valence N
synchronous positive 430
faster positive 425
slower positive 405
synchronous negative 410
faster negative 415
slower negative 435

Note: With valence collapsed (positive = happiness, negative = anger + fear), the design is perfectly balanced: 1260 positive vs 1260 negative trials.

2. Descriptives

VAS by condition

df |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(M = round(mean(vas_value), 2),
            SD = round(sd(vas_value), 2),
            n = n()) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
condition M SD n
synchronous -5.00 46.10 840
faster -5.14 45.58 840
slower -5.93 45.20 840

VAS by valence

df |>
  group_by(valence) |>
  summarise(M = round(mean(vas_value), 2),
            SD = round(sd(vas_value), 2),
            n = n()) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
valence M SD n
positive 22.94 38.37 1260
negative -33.66 32.97 1260

VAS by condition × valence

df |>
  group_by(condition, valence) |>
  summarise(M = round(mean(vas_value), 2),
            SD = round(sd(vas_value), 2),
            n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
condition valence M SD n

synchronous negative -34.06 34.62 410

faster negative -33.22 32.28 415
slower positive 23.91 37.60 405
slower negative -33.71 32.10 435

  group_by(condition, valence, trial_in_mini) |>
  summarise(M  = round(mean(vas_value), 2),

            n  = n(), .groups = "drop") |>

3. Sanity check — intensity × VAS

VAS ratings should correlate strongly with stimulus intensity within each valence.

df |>
  group_by(valence) |>
  summarise(
    rho = round(cor(intensity, vas_value, method = "spearman"), 3),
    p   = round(cor.test(intensity, vas_value,
                         method = "spearman")$p.value, 6),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
valence rho p
positive 0.749 0
negative -0.594 0 
df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = intensity, y = vas_value, color = valence)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence) +
  labs(title = "VAS ~ stimulus intensity per valence",
       x = "Intensity (0–20)", y = "VAS") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

4. Main analysis

Model: vas_value ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity + (1 | participant_id)

lmm_main <- lmer(
  vas_value ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

ANOVA table (Type III)

bold_anova(lmm_main)
Effect Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value Pr(>F)
condition 236.901 118.450 2 2501.0 0.0949 0.9095
valence 2016873.024 2016873.024 1 2501.0 1616.1734 < .001
trial_in_mini_c 534.282 534.282 1 2501.0 0.4281 0.5130
intensity 41803.430 41803.430 1 2501.0 33.4982 < .001
condition:valence 351.224 175.612 2 2501.6 0.1407 0.8687
condition:trial_in_mini_c 682.141 341.071 2 2501.0 0.2733 0.7609
valence:trial_in_mini_c 1307.037 1307.037 1 2502.3 1.0474 0.3062
condition:valence:trial_in_mini_c 3549.750 1774.875 2 2502.4 1.4223 0.2414

Fixed effects

bold_fixef(lmm_main)
Term Estimate Std. Error df t value Pr(>&#124;t&#124;)
(Intercept) 17.6410 2.5965 21.639 6.794 < .001

conditionslower 1.0868 2.4489 2501.435 0.444 0.6572
valencenegative -56.7634 2.4411 2501.735 -23.253 < .001
trial_in_mini_c 0.9144 0.9771 2501.510 0.936 0.3494
intensity 0.6478 0.1119 2501.002 5.788 < .001
conditionfaster:valencenegative 1.0994 3.4517 2501.814 0.319 0.7501
conditionslower:valencenegative -0.7182 3.4538 2501.858 -0.208 0.8353
conditionfaster:trial_in_mini_c -1.6266 1.3908 2501.694 -1.170 0.2423
conditionslower:trial_in_mini_c -1.5748 1.4259 2501.745 -1.104 0.2695
valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c -0.9353 1.4330 2502.069 -0.653 0.5140
conditionfaster:valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c 3.4062 2.0258 2502.405 1.681 0.0928
conditionslower:valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c 1.9375 2.0262 2502.412 0.956 0.3390

5. Post-hoc — LMM split by condition

Tests whether the valence × trial_in_mini interaction is significant within each condition separately.

split_results <- map_dfr(levels(df$condition), function(cond) {
  sub <- df |> filter(condition == cond)
  m <- tryCatch(
    lmer(vas_value ~ valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
           (1 | participant_id),
         data = sub, REML = TRUE,
         control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(m)) {
    a <- anova(m, type = 3)
    row <- a["valence:trial_in_mini_c", ]
    tibble(
      Condition = cond,
      F         = round(row$`F value`, 3),
      df_num    = row$NumDF,
      df_den    = round(row$DenDF, 1),
      p         = round(row$`Pr(>F)`, 4)
    )
  }
})

split_results |>
  kable(caption = "valence x trial_in_mini interaction per condition") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(split_results$p < 0.05), bold = TRUE)
valence x trial_in_mini interaction per condition
Condition F df_num df_den p
synchronous 0.300 1 831.5 0.5839
faster 3.013 1 832.4 0.0830
slower 0.546 1 832.6 0.4602

6. Exposure effect — linear regressions

Direction and magnitude of the trial-by-trial exposure effect for each condition × valence cell. Positive beta = VAS increases over trials within the block.

results <- df |>
  group_by(condition, valence) |>
  summarise(
    beta = round(coef(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))[2], 4),
    r2   = round(summary(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))$r.squared, 4),
    p    = round(summary(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))$coefficients[2, 4], 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

results |>
  kable(caption = "Linear regression: trial_in_mini -> VAS per condition x valence") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(results$p < 0.05), bold = TRUE)
Linear regression: trial_in_mini -> VAS per condition x valence
condition valence beta r2 p
synchronous positive 0.8131 0.0014 0.4386
synchronous negative 0.2635 0.0002 0.7976
faster positive -0.8060 0.0012 0.4681
faster negative 1.4584 0.0058 0.1219
slower positive -0.8246 0.0014 0.4560
slower negative 0.3787 0.0004 0.6727

7. Secondary analysis — emotion (3 levels)

Note: anger and fear use different actor identities — interpret anger vs fear contrasts with caution (possible face-identity confound).

lmm_emo <- lmer(
  vas_value ~ condition * emotion * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)
bold_anova(lmm_emo)
Effect Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value Pr(>F)
condition 277.916 138.958 2 2495.1 0.1113 0.8946
emotion 2009852.457 1004926.228 2 2495.0 805.2131 < .001
trial_in_mini_c 1702.175 1702.175 1 2495.2 1.3639 0.2430
intensity 42712.051 42712.051 1 2495.0 34.2237 < .001
condition:emotion 3343.253 835.813 4 2496.2 0.6697 0.6130
condition:trial_in_mini_c 639.448 319.724 2 2495.1 0.2562 0.7740
emotion:trial_in_mini_c 4331.020 2165.510 2 2496.6 1.7351 0.1766
condition:emotion:trial_in_mini_c 4374.992 1093.748 4 2496.2 0.8764 0.4772

8. Visualisations

Exposure effect

df |>
  group_by(condition, valence, trial_in_mini) |>
  summarise(M  = mean(vas_value),
            SE = sd(vas_value) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = trial_in_mini, y = M,
             color = condition, group = condition)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE, fill = condition),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence, labeller = labeller(
    valence = c(positive = "Positive faces (happiness)",
                negative = "Negative faces (anger + fear)"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6) +
  labs(title = "Exposure effect of FFB on VAS over trial position within block",
       subtitle = "",
       x = "Trial position in block (1–6)",
       y = "Mean VAS ± SE",
       color = "Condition", fill = "Condition") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

VAS per condition × valence

df |>
  group_by(participant_id, condition, valence) |>
  summarise(vas_m = mean(vas_value), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = vas_m, fill = condition)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.4, trim = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar", width = 0.4,
               color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence) +
  labs(title = "VAS per condition × valence",
       x = "Condition", y = "VAS (mean per participant)",
       caption = "Crossbar = grand mean; dots = individual participants") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

Exposure beta per condition × valence (replica Table 2)

results |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = beta, fill = valence)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Exposure effect (beta) per condition × valence",
       subtitle = "Positive = VAS increases over trials within block; negative = decreases",
       x = "Condition", y = "Beta (trial position → VAS)",
       fill = "Valence") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

8. Reaction Times

Descriptives

df |>
  group_by(condition, valence) |>
  summarise(M  = round(mean(rt), 3),
            SD = round(sd(rt), 3),
            n  = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  kable(caption = "RT per condition × valence") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
RT per condition × valence
condition valence M SD n
synchronous positive 1.448 1.103 430
synchronous negative 1.485 2.017 410
faster positive 1.247 0.807 425
faster negative 1.198 0.768 415
slower positive 1.304 0.886 405
slower negative 1.218 0.792 435

LMM — RT

Model: rt ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity + (1 | participant_id)

lmm_rt <- lmer(
  rt ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)
bold_anova(lmm_rt)
Effect Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value Pr(>F)
condition 29.414 14.707 2 2501.0 12.9682 < .001
valence 0.692 0.692 1 2501.0 0.6102 0.4348
trial_in_mini_c 3.073 3.073 1 2501.0 2.7098 0.0999
intensity 0.448 0.448 1 2501.0 0.3949 0.5298
condition:valence 1.829 0.915 2 2501.1 0.8064 0.4466
condition:trial_in_mini_c 0.153 0.076 2 2501.0 0.0674 0.9348
valence:trial_in_mini_c 0.629 0.629 1 2501.1 0.5545 0.4566
condition:valence:trial_in_mini_c 6.541 3.270 2 2501.2 2.8837 0.0561

Fixed effects (RT)

bold_fixef(lmm_rt)
Term Estimate Std. Error df t value Pr(>&#124;t&#124;)

conditionslower:valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c 0.0981 0.0611 2501.163 1.605 0.1086

Exposure effect on RT

Does RT change over trial position within the block?
A negative beta = faster responses over the course of the block (practice effect).
A positive beta = slower responses (fatigue or increasing uncertainty).

results_rt <- df |>
  group_by(condition, valence) |>
  summarise(
    beta = round(coef(lm(rt ~ trial_in_mini))[2], 4),
    r2   = round(summary(lm(rt ~ trial_in_mini))$r.squared, 4),
    p    = round(summary(lm(rt ~ trial_in_mini))$coefficients[2, 4], 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

results_rt |>
  kable(caption = "Linear regression: trial position -> RT per condition x valence") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(results_rt$p < 0.05), bold = TRUE)
Linear regression: trial position -> RT per condition x valence
condition valence beta r2 p
synchronous positive 0.0339 0.0029 0.2674
synchronous negative -0.0810 0.0045 0.1756
faster positive -0.0449 0.0093 0.0469
faster negative 0.0163 0.0013 0.4687
slower positive -0.0393 0.0056 0.1313
slower negative -0.0110 0.0006 0.6192

RT plots

df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = rt, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = 21) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence) +
  labs(title = "RT per condition × valence",
       x = "Condition", y = "RT (s)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

df |>
  group_by(condition, valence, trial_in_mini) |>
  summarise(M  = mean(rt),
            SE = sd(rt) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = trial_in_mini, y = M,
             color = condition, group = condition)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE, fill = condition),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence, labeller = labeller(
    valence = c(positive = "Positive faces (happiness)",
                negative = "Negative faces (anger + fear)"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6) +
  labs(title = "RT over trial position within block",
       x = "Trial position in block (1–6)",
       y = "Mean RT ± SE (s)",
       color = "Condition", fill = "Condition") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

df <- df |>
  group_by(participant_id) |>
  mutate(rt_z = as.numeric(scale(rt))) |>
  ungroup()

outliers_rt <- df |> filter(rt_z > 3)

N outliers RT (>3 SD within participant): 43 / 2520 (1.7%)

outliers_rt |>
  select(participant_id, condition, emotion, rt, rt_z) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3))) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
